---
title: 'Malaise... Listless...'
date: '2016-07-02T20:29:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '6168939925295345382'
blogger_filename: '/2016/07/malaise-listless.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Renewing the Mind (Stinking Thinking)'
  - 'Recovery'
  - 'In The Mire'
  - 'Emotions'
---

<h3><br />When I thought about my mood lately... the words Malaise and Listless came to mind. So I looked them up:</h3><div><br /></div><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malaise" target="_blank">maÂ·laise</a></b>&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>NOUN ::&nbsp;a general feeling of discomfort, illness, or uneasiness whose exact cause is difficult to identify:&nbsp;</i></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>Malaise is a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Non-specific_symptom">non-specific symptom</a> and can present in the slightest ailment, such as an <b><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Emotion">emotion</a> </b>(causing fainting, a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vasovagal_response">vasovagal response</a>) or hunger (light <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hypoglycemia">hypoglycemia</a>), to the most serious conditions (<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cancer">cancer</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stroke">stroke</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heart_attack">heart attack</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internal_bleeding">internal bleeding</a>, etc.).&nbsp;</i></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>Malaise expresses a patient's uneasiness that "something is not right" that may need a medical examination to determine the significance.</i></blockquote><div style="text-align: center;"><b>********</b></div><div style="text-align: center;"><b><br /></b></div><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b><a href="http://www.dictionary.com/browse/listless" target="_blank">listÂ·less</a></b>&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>ADJECTIVE ::&nbsp;</i><i>(of a person or their manner) lacking energy or enthusiasm:&nbsp;"bouts of listless depression"</i>&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>having or showing little or no interest in anything; languid; spiritless; indifferent:&nbsp;a listless mood; a listless handshake.</i></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><a href="https://1828.mshaffer.com/d/word/listless"><b>listless</b></a><br /><i>LIST'LESS, a. Not listening; not attending; indifferent to what is passing; heedless; inattentive; thoughtless; careless; as a listless hearer or spectator.</i></blockquote><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align: center;"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/directionless-i-have-no-direction-graeme-maclean-685c0eebb183.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img border="0" height="320" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/directionless-i-have-no-direction-graeme-maclean-4b87e6a53262.jpg" width="213" /></a></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;"><a href="https://www.flickr.com/photos/gee01/6893884242/in/photolist-FT99Fg-6fxWvP-bvbXB9-6Ukos3-MwR6w-5DrGjW-dLPui6-EYS9q-EYRZC-b5qsqz-6acFzf" target="_blank">directionless I have no direction. By Graeme Maclean&nbsp;</a></td></tr></tbody></table><div><br /></div><div><br /></div>I think of the phrase "the ship is listing to the right"... meaning that the direction the ship is taking toward the right is a meandering unintentional wondering to the right.<br /><br />Leaning if you will.<br /><br />The Captain did not steer that direction, the ship just leaned that way.<br /><br />Imagine you walked down the sidewalk blindfolded, and you gradually walked more and more to the right until you stepped off the curb into the street.<br /><br />"Mal" bad or evil... Malaise... Ill feeling... or a general feeling of discomfort whose exact cause is difficult to identify.<br /><br />I think the text lingo would be "Meh".<br /><br />Why is life stacked against us? Because we have an enemy.<br /><br />Did God give us the tools (prayer, his word, his blood, his Spirit, his power) to fight the enemy? Yes He did.<br /><br />Does that fight take sacrifice (being selfless, getting up early to pray before the day starts, putting aside your feelings and doing what's right for others, etc)? Yes.<br /><br />Is that hard work? You Bet!<br /><br />Is it worth it? I guess so...<br /><br />And.... therein lies the problem. I know all the right answers... but if I don't feel that it's worth it... I don't do all that.<br /><br />Maybe I'm still too worked up into "works"; those things are not what we do to GET victory, those are the things we do when we realize we already have victory.<br /><br />Sounds good on paper... doesn't make me feel better.<br /><br />Time to go to church...<br /><br />Darrell<br /><br /><br />
